frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre I wasn't forcing anything, yaar. When she first said no I thought maybe she's just being formal, but once I found out she's already with someone, I backed off immediately — I respect that. If a girl is single only then it makes sense to try properly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker treats the woman's own refusal as negotiable but respects her unavailability only once another man's claim is established. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring direct disinterest until learning she is 'taken,' framed as being respectful.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
